name: Zotero Bridge MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server bridging AI agents with Zotero — the free, open-source reference management software for academic research. Enables agents to search and retrieve references, manage bibliography collections, access notes and annotations, and integrate Zotero's research library into AI writing and research workflows.
use_cases: Searching Zotero library for references on a topic from AI research assistant agents
Retrieving citation metadata and abstracts from Zotero for AI writing assistance
Managing Zotero collections and tags from AI-assisted research organization agents
Generating bibliographies from Zotero library entries in AI academic writing workflows
not_for: Users without Zotero installed and configured
Mendeley, EndNote, or other reference managers (Zotero-specific)
Commercial citation databases (Zotero manages your personal library)
best_when: An academic researcher or student using Zotero for reference management wants AI writing assistants to access their literature library for citations, summaries, and research support.
avoid_when: You don't use Zotero. Requires Zotero local API enabled (Zotero desktop must be running).

auth.notes: Zotero local API key or Zotero Web API key. Local API requires Zotero desktop running. Web API for Zotero cloud sync access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Zotero free: 300MB storage; unlimited local storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Zotero storage plans for more cloud sync (2GB-unlimited)
pricing.notes: Zotero software is free open source. MCP server is free open source. Storage plans optional.

performance.rate_limits: Zotero local API: no rate limits; Zotero Web API: 100 req/5s

known_agent_gotchas: Requires Zotero desktop application running for local API access
Zotero local API must be enabled in preferences and API key generated
Large Zotero libraries with thousands of items may be slow to search
Community implementation — may not support all Zotero API endpoints
agent_readiness.security_notes: Personal research library may contain sensitive academic materials. Local API is localhost-only. Web API uses HTTPS. Research notes are private.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local API (localhost) or HTTPS Web API. Research content may be sensitive. API key required.
